summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting, starting late at 7:50 PM due to a prior meeting running over. Key actions included granting a utility easement to Duke Energy for emergency generator power at the fire station, adding Juneteenth as a paid holiday for township employees, approving use of American Recovery Plan funds under the standard deduction method, adopting an alternate local government fund apportionment method, approving an indigent burial, denying a mowing bid with plans to rebid, appointing a police chaplain, and approving a zone change resolution for properties on Main Street.
decisions: Approved amendment to agenda adding item C1 (Duke Energy utility easement resolution R-010-2022)
Approved payment of bills for February 9-22, 2022
Approved amendment to Township Employee Policy 6.0 adding June 19th (Juneteenth) as a paid holiday per ORC 511.10
Approved Resolution R-010-2022 granting permanent utility easement and temporary construction easement to Duke Energy of Ohio
Approved use of American Recovery Plan (ARP) funds using standard deduction for continuance of government services per U.S. Treasury Final Rule
Approved Resolution R-009-2022 authorizing alternate method of apportionment of undivided local government funds
Approved indigent burial of Vence Neal Coogan at a cost of $703
Denied mowing bid and approved rebidding
Approved non-sworn position appointment (details unclear from transcript)
Appointed Aaron Ellerman as police chaplain
Approved Resolution R-008-2022 approving zone change for parcels 112214.096 and 112214.097 on Main Street
action_items: Harry to get updates on new stormwater permit conversation from County Township Association meeting
Rebid mowing contract after denying current bid
Make additional phone calls to find mowing service providers
Mr. Haas granted leave of absence from BZA until October 2022 - revisit at that time
Laura to handle signing of Duke Energy easement documents
key_amounts: $800,000+ received in 2021 from American Recovery Plan funds
$703 for indigent burial of Vence Neal Coogan
$50,000 received by City of Milford from local government fund (comparison example)
notable_topics: Duke Energy utility easement for emergency generator power at fire station
Addition of Juneteenth (June 19th) as paid township employee holiday
American Recovery Plan (ARP) fund usage under U.S. Treasury Final Rule standard deduction
Local government fund alternate apportionment method (Resolution R-009-2022)
BZA member Mr. Haas leave of absence
Appointment of Aaron Ellerman as police chaplain
Zone change approval for Main Street properties (R-008-2022)
attendees: Brian Shrive (Assistant Prosecutor, Chief of Civil Division)
Mr. Haas (BZA member taking leave of absence)
notes: Meeting started 50 minutes late at 7:50 PM due to a prior meeting running over. Boy Scouts were present observing for community service. Transcript quality is poor with numerous garbled/corrupted passages. Brian Shrive and Julia Carney from the Clermont County Prosecutor's Civil Division visited to introduce themselves and remind the board of their availability.
title: BOT Mtg 2.22.22.mp4
